theme: Self-tuning black-box optimizer over a mix of smooth-unimodal and rugged-multimodal functions under a query budget.
title: Ridge-Blind Prospecting: Self-Tuning a Black-Box Landscape Scan
innovation_hook: Spend a few structured probes to estimate the landscape's correlation length, then set exploration step and restart aggressiveness from it -- small steps for smooth, elite-seeded restarts for rugged.
strategies: trivial: ignore every pilot reading, spend the whole drilling budget as fixed-seed uniform-random points across the box (reproduces the evaluator's own weak blind-sweep reference)

greedy (the trap): trust only the neighbourhood you started in -- pick the best of the start point and the short-hop local probes taken right around it, then drill a fixed-radius jittered cluster of wells around that single point, never looking at the coarse scan_probes -- on instances where the tallest deposit sits elsewhere in the box (only visible via scan_probes) this caps out at the secondary deposit it started next to, however precisely it refines there

strong: use the local probes to estimate the field's correlation length (how fast value changes over short hops near the start); if the field looks smooth, seed one tight small-step cluster around the single best pilot reading; if it looks rugged, don't trust the local neighbourhood alone -- seed several elite-restart clusters from the best pilot readings across the WHOLE box (including scan_probes near distant deposits) with a wider step size per cluster
alternatives: pure hill-climbing multi-start from every pilot reading with a fixed step regardless of ruggedness, or a wider blind low-discrepancy sweep that never exploits the pilot readings at all
